hypothesis: If an autonomous materials discovery agent in MADE uses the pretrained MatterGen generator with diversity-based acquisition and initializes from all known stable structures, then across Na–O and Co–Nb–Sn it will discover more novel stable compounds within a 20-query budget than an agent using random generation with random acquisition from elemental end-members, because generative priors and diversity-aware selection more efficiently target low-energy, compositionally diverse regions of the search space.
